notes.anchor: Plancius keeps me here by his generosity
notes.note: Cn. Plancius, the quaestor of Macedonia, sheltered Cicero through his exile and pressed him to stay; here at Dyrrachium he hopes the two may quit the province together.
Plancius's quaestorian term was ending; he hoped to escort Cicero home at his own departure, which he reckoned would do him honour.
P. Cornelius Lentulus Spinther, consul-elect for 57 BC and the chief champion of Cicero's recall; his goodwill is Cicero's best lever on Pompey.
Q. Caecilius Metellus Nepos, the other consul of 57 BC and a former enemy of Cicero, whose hostility Atticus was working to soften.

notes.anchor: the sixth day before the Kalends of December
notes.note: The Roman calendar counted backward and inclusively from fixed points; the Kalends fell on the first of the month, so this dates the letter to 26 November 58 BC.
